title: Will President Trump send ICE agents to Boston’s Logan Airport?
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: President Trump plans to send ICE agents to airports to arrest illegal immigrants unless DHS funding is restored.
Boston's Logan Airport has not faced major disruptions and ICE deployment there remains uncertain, TSA officials say.
Over 400 TSA workers resigned nationwide due to the government shutdown, affecting airport security staffing.

title: Is Bruins goalie Jeremy Swayman a legitimate Vezina candidate? The numbers speak for themselves.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jeremy Swayman made 42 saves, securing a crucial 4-2 Bruins victory over the Red Wings and boosting playoff chances.
Swayman leads the Bruins with a .930 save percentage since returning from the Olympic break, vital for their playoff push.
Swayman ranks second in the NHL with 27.1 goals saved above expected, highlighting his Vezina Trophy candidacy.

title: Celtics legend Paul Pierce continues to make a case for Jaylen Brown as NBA MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Paul Pierce argues Jaylen Brown deserves NBA MVP consideration due to his stellar play for the Celtics.
With Brown's leadership, the Celtics are second in the Eastern Conference, posting a 47-23 record.
Brown is averaging career-highs in points, rebounds, and assists, while also excelling defensively.

title: Plymouth police officer on paid leave amid criminal investigation
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Plymouth police officer Samantha Pelrine is on paid leave amid a criminal investigation by the Massachusetts State Police.
The investigation into Pelrine concerns her conduct while off duty, with Plymouth police also conducting an internal review.
Details of the investigation remain undisclosed, with the Plymouth County District Attorney's office declining to comment further.

title: MLB writer predicts Roman Anthony will win AL MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Athletic's Eno Sarris predicts Red Sox's Roman Anthony will win the 2026 AL MVP award.
Anthony impressed at the 2026 World Baseball Classic, batting .280 with two home runs and seven RBI.
In 2025, Anthony ended his rookie MLB season with a .292 average, eight home runs, and 32 RBIs over 71 games.

title: Pedestrian killed in Roxbury hit-and-run
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A pedestrian was killed in a hit-and-run accident Friday morning near Southampton and Bradston streets in Roxbury.
The driver fled the scene, but the involved vehicle was recovered by Boston Police later.
No arrests have been made yet as Boston Police continue their investigation into the incident.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about chances for snow through Monday
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Rain expected to transition to wet snow across Massachusetts, Connecticut, and Rhode Island early Monday morning.
National Weather Service forecasts up to 2 inches of wet snow, especially near Interstate 90.
Meteorologists predict snow showers could result in a coating to an inch by Monday night.

title: Mass. police officer arrested for OUI, department says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Norwood Police Officer Shawn Willman was arrested Thursday for operating a vehicle under the influence of alcohol.
Willman was off duty during the incident and has been placed on administrative leave pending investigation.
An internal affairs investigation is underway, and legal proceedings will determine his employment status.

title: Police: Mass. man allegedly stabbed friend during fight over woman at Miami Beach bachelor party
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Olajuwon Shawn Dickerson, 32, from Cape Cod, was charged with aggravated battery after allegedly stabbing a friend in Miami Beach.
The incident occurred during a bachelor party; police found Dickerson and victim Brian Gordon with serious injuries.
Court orders prohibit Dickerson from contacting Gordon; both men were hospitalized, and a knife was recovered.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-1 loss to St. Louis City SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution lost 3-1 to St. Louis City SC, marking their third defeat in four 2026 season games.
Revolution's Peyton Miller was absent from the starting lineup due to injury concerns but is expected to return soon.
New England forward Leo Campana suffered an undisclosed injury during the match, conditions to be evaluated with an MRI.
